senses.gloss: a male given name from English, equivalent to English John

examples.text: وَشِمِلَّةٍ حَرْفٍ كَأَنَّ قُتُودَهَا … جَلَّلْتُهَا (var. لَبَسْتُهَا) جَوْنَ السَّرَاةِ خَفَيْدَدًا
examples.english: Some hardy she-camel the saddle-woods of which I applied like upon a black-backed ostrich

etymology.text:
Equivalent to English lagoon, via a Romance source with the la- part interpreted as article.

From English John.

From Middle Persian [script needed] (gwn' /⁠gōn⁠/, “colour, complexion”), equalling Classical Persian گون (gūn).
